page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.medium is a compute-optimized instance featuring a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical core based on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, this instance is provisioned with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 instruction/data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography enabled. It does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-threaded capabilities. The single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1516.59 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1500.0, both falling into the average category. However, because the instance is constrained to a single vCPU, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core (1516.59) and Geekbench multi-core (1502.0) rank in the bottom 10% of tested servers. Memory performance is also limited, with a read-only large block bandwidth of 31.16 GB/sec and a write bandwidth of 11.03 GB/sec.

This instance is designed for network-optimized, low-intensity workloads that benefit from a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. It is suitable for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, or small-scale utility scripts. Due to its single-core limitation and low memory capacity, it is not suited for multi-threaded compilation, database operations, or large-scale LLM inference. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7gn.medium offers a low-cost entry point into the Graviton7 family, providing dedicated ARM resources and high network throughput relative to its small hardware footprint.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.medium is a compute-optimized, ARM-based instance powered by a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 core running at 2.6 GHz. It features 2.0 GB of RAM, a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and relies on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1500.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1516.59. However, due to its single-vCPU configuration, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower percentiles, with a large-block memory write speed of 11.03 GB/sec. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level ARM development, lightweight single-threaded tasks, and basic network-optimized utility workloads, but it is not suitable for resource-intensive database operations or multi-threaded applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.medium is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 1 dedicated Graviton vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.medium is a 1-vCPU ARM64 instance with 2.0 GB RAM and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for lightweight single-core tasks.
tagline: A single-core ARM64 instance offering dedicated Graviton compute and optimized network bandwidth for lightweight, single-threaded utility workloads.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 core running at 2.6 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory with a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average single-core performance with 1500.0 Geekbench single-core score
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic network utilities
